notes: Smallest tool-less round-trip isolating the TERMINAL-TEXT end signal: one user prompt produces a final plain-text assistant message (no tool_use) and pi's stopReason=stop maps to turn_done, settling working→ready. Overlaps basic-turn intentionally — this cell focuses on the end-of-turn mechanism, not the broader lifecycle. WHY interactive (not headless `pi --print`): headless pi is single-shot and exits with process_exited almost immediately after the reply, so the terminal working→ready settle races teardown and the arc isn't cleanly observable (concern C6). The pi interactive driver (drive-pi-interactive.sh) keeps the rollout REPL alive across the single send+wait_turn, with a trailing sleep so the daemon observes the working→ready settle as a distinct arc before the tmux session is killed once → a SINGLE process_exited. C7 / SESSION-IDENTITY: the live REPL emits a transient proc-<PID> pre-session born ready at launch (it bridges the gap until the UUID-keyed rollout transcript file appears), so session_birth — which only asserts 'a session appears in ready within 2s of launch' — legitimately matches that pre-session. The TURN ARC, by contrast, must land on the UUID rollout session: turn_start anchors to recording start with NO session pin so the matcher picks the FIRST working in the whole recording, which is necessarily the rollout session (the pre-session never goes working); turn_end, pid_bind, and teardown then pin to turn_start's session via same_session_as, so a co-occurring pre-session can never satisfy them. HOW runs differ: exact assistant wording, per-turn token counts, and sub-second timing vary (model-output variance); pi may emit MORE than one working→ready arc if the single prompt produces multiple transcript-activity bursts — the spec asserts the first arc closes to ready on terminal text and never enters waiting, not an exact arc count. Stable structurally: a single UUID rollout session, one ready→working→ready arc closing on terminal text, no waiting transition, and a single process_exited at teardown.

text: A session appears in ready within 2s of REPL launch
User prompt → working (binds the rollout session; the pre-session never enters working)
Final plain-text assistant message closes the turn → back to ready; state never entered waiting
PID bound to the rollout session
REPL torn down once after the turn → a single process_exited on the rollout session; state immediately before teardown is ready
